CloudFormation → OCI Terraform Translation
================================================================================

Output: output/automated-translations/web-app-stack
================================================================================

✓ Written: output/automated-translations/web-app-stack/variables.tf
✓ Written: output/automated-translations/web-app-stack/resource.tf
✓ Written: output/automated-translations/web-app-stack/outputs.tf
✓ Written: output/automated-translations/web-app-stack/migration-notes.txt
✓ terraform fmt: formatted successfully

================================================================================
TRANSLATION SUMMARY
================================================================================
Total resources:    14
Translated:         10
Success rate:       71.4%
Unsupported:        4
Warnings:           9
Migration notes:    20
================================================================================

  - DBSubnetGroup (AWS::RDS::DBSubnetGroup)
  - ALB (AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer)
  - TargetGroup (AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup)
  - Listener (AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener)

✓ Translation complete! Review output/automated-translations/web-app-stack/migration-notes.txt for manual steps.

[WARN] Cannot auto-populate vcn_id for ECSCluster
[WARN] Missing required property 'kubernetes_version' for ECSCluster
[WARN] No OCI mapping for AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::LoadBalancer (aws_load_balancer)
[WARN] No OCI mapping for AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::TargetGroup (aws_target_group)
[WARN] No OCI mapping for AWS::ElasticLoadBalancingV2::Listener (aws_listener)

interactions.full_output: {"decision": "APPROVED_WITH_NOTES", "confidence_override": null, "architectural_mismatch": false, "issues": [{"id": 1, "severity": "CRITICAL", "category": "completeness", "resource": "N/A (ECS TaskDefinition / Service)", "description": "ECS TaskDefinition and ECS Service have no OCI Terraform resource equivalents. The translation correctly identifies this as a critical gap but does not include any Terraform kubernetes_deployment or kubernetes_service resources (or Helm release) to actually deploy the application workload on OKE. The infrastructure will be created but no application will run.", "recommendation": "Add a kubernetes_deployment and kubernetes_service resource using the Terraform 'kubernetes' or 'helm' provider, configured to connect to the OKE cluster after creation. Alternatively, provide a complete post-apply script or Helm chart with explicit instructions. Without this, the migration is incomplete."}, {"id": 2, "severity": "HIGH", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_database_autonomous_database.app_db", "description": "RDS PostgreSQL 15.4 (db.t3.medium, 50GB) is mapped to OCI Autonomous Database. While ADB supports a PostgreSQL-compatible interface, this is a significant architectural change: (1) ADB minimum storage is 1 TB vs 50 GB requested, (2) ADB PostgreSQL compatibility is limited and does not support all extensions, (3) connection semantics differ (mTLS wallet by default), (4) cost implications are substantially higher. A more equivalent mapping would be oci_database_db_system with PostgreSQL or oci_mysql_mysql_db_system (if PostgreSQL isn't strictly required), or a self-managed PostgreSQL on compute.", "recommendation": "Consider using oci_psql_db_system (OCI Database with PostgreSQL) which was GA'd in 2024 and provides a much closer equivalent to RDS PostgreSQL \u2014 supporting standard PostgreSQL wire protocol, extensions, and smaller storage allocations. If ADB is intentionally chosen, document the compatibility trade-offs and cost delta explicitly."}, {"id": 3, "severity": "HIGH", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_core_vcn.main", "description": "The CloudFormation template accepts existing VpcId and SubnetIds as parameters, meaning it deploys into pre-existing networking infrastructure. The OCI translation creates an entirely new VCN and subnets from scratch. This is a significant behavioral difference \u2014 the original stack assumes networking exists, while the translation provisions it.", "recommendation": "Either (a) use data sources (data.oci_core_vcn, data.oci_core_subnet) to reference existing infrastructure with vcn_id and subnet_ids as input variables, matching the CFN pattern, or (b) clearly document that the translation intentionally expands scope to include networking and that users deploying into existing VCNs must modify accordingly."}, {"id": 4, "severity": "HIGH", "category": "property_translation", "resource": "oci_database_autonomous_database.app_db", "description": "The mapping claims 'is_local_data_guard_enabled=true' for MultiAZ equivalent. Local Data Guard on Autonomous Database is an Enterprise feature with significant cost uplift and may not be available on all shapes/regions. Additionally, the backup_retention_period_in_days attribute doesn't exist on oci_database_autonomous_database \u2014 ADB uses automatic backups with a fixed 60-day retention. The CFN BackupRetentionPeriod=7 cannot be directly mapped.", "recommendation": "Verify that is_local_data_guard_enabled is supported on the chosen ADB workload type. Remove the incorrect backup_retention_period_in_days claim from mapping notes. If using oci_psql_db_system instead, backup retention is configurable."}, {"id": 5, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_core_service_gateway.sgw", "description": "The service gateway references data.oci_core_services.all.services[0].id without filtering. The first service returned may not be 'All <region> Services In Oracle Services Network'. This is non-deterministic and could select the wrong service.", "recommendation": "Filter the data source: use a filter block with name = 'All .* Services In Oracle Services Network' or use the specific CIDR label to ensure the correct service is selected."}, {"id": 6, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "completeness", "resource": "oci_load_balancer_listener (HTTPS)", "description": "The translation adds an HTTPS listener that wasn't in the original CFN template (the CFN only had an HTTP listener on port 80 with forward action). While the ALB security group allowed 443, no HTTPS listener or certificate was defined. Adding this is reasonable for production but changes the scope.", "recommendation": "Document clearly that the HTTPS listener is an enhancement over the original CFN template. Make it conditional (as noted) and ensure the ssl_certificate_name variable defaults to empty/null so the base deployment matches CFN behavior."}, {"id": 7, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_kms_vault + oci_kms_key + oci_vault_secret", "description": "The CFN template uses a simple NoEcho parameter for DBPassword \u2014 it has no Secrets Manager or SSM integration. Creating a full KMS Vault + Key + Secret is scope expansion. While it's a security best practice, it adds complexity and cost (KMS vault has associated charges) that wasn't in the original design.", "recommendation": "Consider making the Vault/Secret resources optional (behind a boolean variable). For a direct equivalent of the CFN NoEcho parameter, a sensitive Terraform variable is sufficient. Document this as a security enhancement."}, {"id": 8, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "property_translation", "resource": "oci_logging_log_group + oci_logging_log", "description": "The mapping states retention_duration=30 (OCI minimum). However, oci_logging_log does not have a configurable retention_duration attribute. OCI Logging has a fixed active retention period (currently implementation-dependent). The translation's claim about configuring 30-day retention via a Terraform attribute is incorrect.", "recommendation": "Remove the retention_duration configuration claim. Document that OCI Logging retention is managed by the service. For archival, configure log export to Object Storage with lifecycle rules."}, {"id": 9, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "monitoring", "resource": "oci_monitoring_alarm.high_cpu", "description": "The mapping states 'pending_duration=PT5M' for EvaluationPeriods=2 \u00d7 Period=300s. The actual equivalent would be PT10M (2 evaluation periods of 5 minutes = 10 minutes total). PT5M corresponds to only 1 evaluation period.", "recommendation": "Set pending_duration to 'PT10M' to correctly match the CloudWatch alarm's behavior of 2 consecutive 5-minute periods."}, {"id": 10, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_containerengine_cluster.app_cluster", "description": "The translation uses OKE virtual node pools as the Fargate equivalent. While architecturally reasonable, OKE virtual nodes have limitations: no DaemonSets (affects logging agent deployment), limited container runtime options, and different networking behavior. The architecture notes mention deploying a Fluent Bit DaemonSet which is incompatible with virtual nodes.", "recommendation": "Either (a) use managed nodes instead of virtual nodes to support DaemonSets for logging, or (b) use OCI Logging sidecar containers instead of DaemonSets for log collection on virtual nodes. Resolve the contradiction in the architecture notes."}, {"id": 11, "severity": "LOW", "category": "completeness", "resource": "main_tf_excerpt", "description": "The main.tf excerpt is truncated mid-resource (private route table). The full Terraform configuration is not available for review, making it impossible to verify actual HCL syntax, all resource definitions, variable declarations, and output blocks.", "recommendation": "Provide the complete Terraform configuration including all resource blocks, variables.tf, outputs.tf, and any module definitions for a thorough review."}, {"id": 12, "severity": "LOW", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_artifacts_container_repository.app", "description": "An OCIR repository is created but the original CFN template doesn't define an ECR repository \u2014 it only references an image URI in the task definition. This is scope expansion, though a reasonable one for a complete migration.", "recommendation": "Document as an enhancement. Consider that the image URI in the original task definition may reference a third-party registry, not necessarily ECR."}, {"id": 13, "severity": "LOW", "category": "completeness", "resource": "N/A", "description": "The resource_count claims 42 resources but only ~17 resource mappings are documented. The gap between claimed and documented resources makes it difficult to verify completeness.", "recommendation": "Provide a complete resource inventory or at minimum list all 42 resources with their types and names."}], "approved_notes": "The translation demonstrates strong architectural understanding of the AWS-to-OCI mapping and thoroughly documents gaps and prerequisites. The choice of OKE for ECS Fargate and the 3-tier NSG security model are appropriate. However, several mapping accuracy issues need attention: (1) the RDS PostgreSQL mapping should consider oci_psql_db_system as a closer equivalent to RDS PostgreSQL, (2) the Kubernetes workload gap needs a concrete solution (kubernetes provider resources or Helm chart), and (3) several property-level claims in the mapping notes are inaccurate (ADB backup retention, log retention attributes, alarm pending_duration). The VCN scope expansion from parameter-based to fully provisioned is significant and should be clearly flagged to users.", "review_summary": "This is a well-structured and thoroughly documented translation that correctly identifies the major architectural patterns and critical gaps in the ECS-to-OKE migration. The most significant concern is the RDS PostgreSQL to Autonomous Database mapping \u2014 OCI's newer PostgreSQL Database Service (oci_psql_db_system) would be a much more accurate and cost-effective equivalent. The translation is comprehensive in scope (adding Vault, OCIR, monitoring beyond the original CFN) but several property-level claims in the mapping notes contain inaccuracies that could mislead implementers.", "_confidence": 0.4549999999999999, "_duration": 59.22819957602769}
issues_found: [CRITICAL] ECS TaskDefinition and ECS Service have no OCI Terraform resource equivalents. The translation correctly identifies this as a critical gap but does not include any Terraform kubernetes_deployment or kubernetes_service resources (or Helm release) to actually deploy the application workload on OKE. The infrastructure will be created but no application will run.

[HIGH] RDS PostgreSQL 15.4 (db.t3.medium, 50GB) is mapped to OCI Autonomous Database. While ADB supports a PostgreSQL-compatible interface, this is a significant architectural change: (1) ADB minimum storage is 1 TB vs 50 GB requested, (2) ADB PostgreSQL compatibility is limited and does not support all extensions, (3) connection semantics differ (mTLS wallet by default), (4) cost implications are substantially higher. A more equivalent mapping would be oci_database_db_system with PostgreSQL or oci_mysql_mysql_db_system (if PostgreSQL isn't strictly required), or a self-managed PostgreSQL on compute.

[HIGH] The CloudFormation template accepts existing VpcId and SubnetIds as parameters, meaning it deploys into pre-existing networking infrastructure. The OCI translation creates an entirely new VCN and subnets from scratch. This is a significant behavioral difference — the original stack assumes networking exists, while the translation provisions it.

[HIGH] The mapping claims 'is_local_data_guard_enabled=true' for MultiAZ equivalent. Local Data Guard on Autonomous Database is an Enterprise feature with significant cost uplift and may not be available on all shapes/regions. Additionally, the backup_retention_period_in_days attribute doesn't exist on oci_database_autonomous_database — ADB uses automatic backups with a fixed 60-day retention. The CFN BackupRetentionPeriod=7 cannot be directly mapped.

[MEDIUM] The service gateway references data.oci_core_services.all.services[0].id without filtering. The first service returned may not be 'All <region> Services In Oracle Services Network'. This is non-deterministic and could select the wrong service.

[MEDIUM] The translation adds an HTTPS listener that wasn't in the original CFN template (the CFN only had an HTTP listener on port 80 with forward action). While the ALB security group allowed 443, no HTTPS listener or certificate was defined. Adding this is reasonable for production but changes the scope.

[MEDIUM] The CFN template uses a simple NoEcho parameter for DBPassword — it has no Secrets Manager or SSM integration. Creating a full KMS Vault + Key + Secret is scope expansion. While it's a security best practice, it adds complexity and cost (KMS vault has associated charges) that wasn't in the original design.

[MEDIUM] The mapping states retention_duration=30 (OCI minimum). However, oci_logging_log does not have a configurable retention_duration attribute. OCI Logging has a fixed active retention period (currently implementation-dependent). The translation's claim about configuring 30-day retention via a Terraform attribute is incorrect.

[MEDIUM] The mapping states 'pending_duration=PT5M' for EvaluationPeriods=2 × Period=300s. The actual equivalent would be PT10M (2 evaluation periods of 5 minutes = 10 minutes total). PT5M corresponds to only 1 evaluation period.

[MEDIUM] The translation uses OKE virtual node pools as the Fargate equivalent. While architecturally reasonable, OKE virtual nodes have limitations: no DaemonSets (affects logging agent deployment), limited container runtime options, and different networking behavior. The architecture notes mention deploying a Fluent Bit DaemonSet which is incompatible with virtual nodes.

[LOW] The main.tf excerpt is truncated mid-resource (private route table). The full Terraform configuration is not available for review, making it impossible to verify actual HCL syntax, all resource definitions, variable declarations, and output blocks.

[LOW] An OCIR repository is created but the original CFN template doesn't define an ECR repository — it only references an image URI in the task definition. This is scope expansion, though a reasonable one for a complete migration.
[LOW] The resource_count claims 42 resources but only ~17 resource mappings are documented. The gap between claimed and documented resources makes it difficult to verify completeness.

fixes_applied: Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes provider to required_providers with version >= 2.23.0 and configured provider block using OCI CLI exec plugin for authentication against the OKE cluster endpoint
Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes_namespace.app to create the application namespace on OKE
Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes_config_map.app_env mapping ECS TaskDefinition Environment variables (DB_HOST resolved from oci_psql_db_system endpoint, DB_PORT, ENVIRONMENT)
Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes_secret.db_password to inject DB password into pods securely

Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes_deployment.app fully mapping ECS TaskDefinition: container image, port 8080, CPU 250m/500m limits, memory 512Mi/1Gi limits, env_from ConfigMap, DB_PASSWORD from Secret, liveness/readiness probes on /health, OCIR imagePullSecret, topology spread constraints for multi-AD HA
Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes_horizontal_pod_autoscaler_v2.app scaling on CPU at 80% (maps HighCPUAlarm threshold), min=app_desired_count, max=app_max_count

Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added kubernetes_service.app of type LoadBalancer with OCI CCM annotations to attach to the pre-created oci_load_balancer_load_balancer and backend set, mapping AppService LoadBalancers configuration
Issue 1 (CRITICAL) – Added app_max_count, k8s_namespace, ocir_pull_secret_name variables to support the new Kubernetes resources
Issue 2 (HIGH) – Replaced oci_database_autonomous_database.app_db entirely with oci_psql_db_system.app_db using the correct GA 2024 resource type for RDS PostgreSQL migration

Issue 2 (HIGH) – oci_psql_db_system uses standard PostgreSQL wire protocol (no wallet/mTLS), 50 GB storage (no forced 1 TB minimum), db_version=15 (maps PostgreSQL 15.4), instance_count=2 across two ADs for MultiAZ HA
Issue 2 (HIGH) – Added db_storage_size_gb variable (default 50, validated >= 50) and db_backup_retention_days variable (default 7, validated 1-35) for explicit control
Issue 2 (HIGH) – Updated all references from oci_database_autonomous_database.app_db to oci_psql_db_system.app_db throughout outputs.tf and kubernetes_config_map

Issue 2 (HIGH) – Removed invalid compute_model, compute_count, data_storage_size_in_tbs, is_local_data_guard_enabled, is_mtls_connection_required, private_endpoint_label, db_license_model, database_edition, db_workload attributes that belong to ADB not oci_psql_db_system
Issue 3 (HIGH) – Added use_existing_vcn boolean variable to control new vs existing networking mode, mirroring CFN VpcId/SubnetIds parameter pattern
Issue 3 (HIGH) – Added existing_vcn_id, existing_public_subnet_ids, existing_private_app_subnet_ids, existing_private_db_subnet_id variables for existing infrastructure mode
Issue 3 (HIGH) – Added data source blocks (data.oci_core_vcn.existing, data.oci_core_subnet.existing_*) with count = var.use_existing_vcn ? 1 : 0

Issue 3 (HIGH) – Converted all managed networking resources (oci_core_vcn, oci_core_internet_gateway, oci_core_nat_gateway, oci_core_service_gateway, oci_core_route_table, oci_core_security_list, oci_core_subnet ×5) to use count = var.use_existing_vcn ? 0 : 1

Issue 3 (HIGH) – Added local values (local.vcn_id, local.public_subnet_1_id, local.public_subnet_2_id, local.private_app_1_id, local.private_app_2_id, local.private_db_id) that resolve to either data source or managed resource IDs based on use_existing_vcn
Issue 3 (HIGH) – Updated all downstream resource references (NSGs, OKE cluster, LB, DB system, OKE node pool) to use local.* values instead of direct resource references
Issue 3 (HIGH) – Updated outputs to use local.* values for VCN and subnet IDs

Issue 4 (HIGH) – Removed invalid backup_retention_period_in_days attribute (does not exist on oci_database_autonomous_database) by replacing with oci_psql_db_system management_policy.backup_policy.retention_days
Issue 4 (HIGH) – Removed is_local_data_guard_enabled attribute and corrected MultiAZ mapping note: oci_psql_db_system uses instance_count=2 for HA, not Data Guard
Issue 4 (HIGH) – Removed db_license_model variable (ADB-specific, not applicable to oci_psql_db_system)
Issue 4 (HIGH) – Corrected all resource mapping notes to accurately reflect oci_psql_db_system attributes and behavior

interactions.full_output: {"decision": "APPROVED_WITH_NOTES", "confidence_override": null, "architectural_mismatch": false, "issues": [{"id": 1, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "completeness", "resource": "kubernetes provider", "description": "The kubernetes provider block directly references oci_containerengine_cluster.app_cluster.id in the exec args, which will fail during the initial `terraform init` / `terraform plan` if the cluster does not yet exist. The two-phase apply is documented in prerequisites, but the provider configuration itself will cause evaluation errors during Phase-1 planning because Terraform evaluates all provider blocks before any resource targeting.", "recommendation": "Use a null/empty provider configuration guarded by a variable (e.g., `var.deploy_k8s_workloads = false`) or split the Kubernetes workloads into a separate Terraform root module/workspace entirely. This avoids the chicken-and-egg problem with provider evaluation."}, {"id": 2, "severity": "HIGH", "category": "completeness", "resource": "kubernetes_deployment.app", "description": "The OCIR image pull secret dependency is noted as a gap but is a hard prerequisite that will cause pod ImagePullBackOff failures if missed. The kubernetes_deployment references `ocir_pull_secret_name` but the secret is not created by Terraform \u2014 it requires a manual kubectl step between phases, making the deployment non-reproducible from Terraform alone.", "recommendation": "Create the OCIR pull secret as a `kubernetes_secret` resource of type `kubernetes.io/dockerconfigjson` within the Terraform configuration itself, sourcing credentials from OCI Vault or variables marked sensitive. This eliminates the manual step and makes the deployment fully declarative."}, {"id": 3, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "iam", "resource": "oci_identity_dynamic_group.oke_pods", "description": "The dynamic group matching rule is compartment-wide (matches ALL instances in the compartment), which is significantly broader than the ECS TaskExecutionRole that only applies to ECS tasks. This is acknowledged in gaps but represents a real security concern \u2014 any compute instance in the compartment gets the same permissions as the application pods.", "recommendation": "Use OKE Workload Identity (available on ENHANCED_CLUSTER) with per-service-account IAM policies. Alternatively, narrow the dynamic group matching rule to match only the OKE cluster's node pool instances using resource tags or the cluster OCID in the matching rule."}, {"id": 4, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_psql_db_system.app_db", "description": "The shape mapping 'PostgreSQL.VM.Standard.E4.Flex.2.32GB' (2 vCPU / 32 GB RAM) is significantly over-provisioned compared to db.t3.medium (2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM). While the CPU count matches, the memory is 8x larger, which will result in higher costs.", "recommendation": "Verify available OCI PostgreSQL DB System shapes and select the smallest shape that matches the 2 vCPU requirement. If PostgreSQL.VM.Standard.E4.Flex supports configurable memory (flex shape), set memory to a lower value such as 8 GB. Document the cost difference in migration notes."}, {"id": 5, "severity": "LOW", "category": "completeness", "resource": "oci_load_balancer_listener.https", "description": "The HTTPS listener is conditionally created based on ssl_certificate_name, but the original CloudFormation template has a listener on port 443. Without the HTTPS listener, the ALB NSG rule allowing port 443 ingress will accept traffic that has no listener to serve it.", "recommendation": "Either make the HTTPS listener mandatory (require ssl_certificate_name) or remove the port 443 NSG ingress rule when HTTPS is not configured, to avoid accepting connections that will be reset. Alternatively, add an HTTP redirect from 443 to 80 as a temporary measure."}, {"id": 6, "severity": "LOW", "category": "property_translation", "resource": "oci_load_balancer_load_balancer.alb", "description": "The flexible shape bandwidth is set to 10-100 Mbps, which may be insufficient for production workloads that the original ALB handled. AWS ALB scales automatically with no predefined bandwidth cap.", "recommendation": "Consider setting a higher maximum bandwidth (e.g., 400 or 8000 Mbps) for the flexible shape, or document the bandwidth limit and monitoring approach so it can be adjusted based on actual traffic patterns."}, {"id": 7, "severity": "LOW", "category": "completeness", "resource": "main_tf_excerpt", "description": "The main.tf excerpt is truncated at the locals block. While the resource_mappings describe all resources comprehensively, the actual HCL for most resources (NSGs, DB system, OKE cluster, node pool, K8s resources, etc.) cannot be syntactically validated from what is provided.", "recommendation": "Provide the complete Terraform configuration for full validation. The architectural mapping and resource selection appear correct based on the detailed descriptions, but HCL syntax and attribute correctness cannot be fully confirmed."}, {"id": 8, "severity": "MEDIUM", "category": "resource_mapping", "resource": "oci_containerengine_cluster.app_cluster", "description": "The kubernetes_service is annotated to attach to the pre-created OCI LB and backend set, which requires OCI Cloud Controller Manager (CCM) specific annotations. If these annotations are incorrect or the CCM version doesn't support this pattern, the integration between the K8s service and the OCI LB may fail. This is a complex integration point that differs significantly from ECS-ALB tight coupling.", "recommendation": "Verify the exact OCI CCM annotations required (e.g., oci.oraclecloud.com/load-balancer-type, service.beta.kubernetes.io/oci-load-balancer-backend-protocol, etc.) against the current OCI CCM documentation. Consider an alternative approach: let the kubernetes_service of type LoadBalancer create the LB automatically via CCM, and remove the separate oci_load_balancer resources."}, {"id": 9, "severity": "LOW", "category": "monitoring", "resource": "oci_monitoring_alarm.high_cpu", "description": "The MQL query targets the oci_containerengine namespace, but for Virtual Node Pools the metric namespace and metric names may differ from standard OKE managed node pools. Virtual Nodes emit metrics through a different path.", "recommendation": "Verify the exact MQL metric namespace and metric name for Virtual Node Pod CPU utilization. It may be `oci_oke_virtual_node` or require Kubernetes Metrics Server / Prometheus-based monitoring instead of native OCI Monitoring."}], "approved_notes": "This is a thorough and well-documented translation that addresses the core architectural mapping between AWS 3-tier (ALB + ECS Fargate + RDS PostgreSQL) and OCI (LB + OKE Virtual Nodes + PostgreSQL DB System). The choice of oci_psql_db_system over Autonomous Database is correct and well-justified. The NSG-to-NSG security model properly mirrors AWS SG-to-SG references. The two-phase apply approach is pragmatic given the kubernetes provider chicken-and-egg constraint. The gaps and migration prerequisites are exceptionally thorough. Key items to address before production: (1) resolve the kubernetes provider evaluation issue for Phase-1, (2) automate the OCIR pull secret creation, (3) narrow the IAM dynamic group scope, and (4) verify the DB shape memory sizing for cost optimization.", "review_summary": "The translation demonstrates strong architectural understanding, correctly mapping ECS Fargate to OKE Virtual Nodes and RDS PostgreSQL to oci_psql_db_system rather than the common mistake of using Autonomous Database. The security group chain is properly translated to NSG-to-NSG rules, and the comprehensive gaps/prerequisites documentation shows production readiness awareness. The main concerns are the kubernetes provider chicken-and-egg problem during Phase-1 apply, the overly broad IAM dynamic group scope, and the significantly over-provisioned database memory (32 GB vs 4 GB in the original). The truncated HCL prevents full syntax validation but the described resource structure and attribute mappings are architecturally sound.", "_confidence": 0.87, "_duration": 44.701417135074735}
issues_found: [MEDIUM] The kubernetes provider block directly references oci_containerengine_cluster.app_cluster.id in the exec args, which will fail during the initial `terraform init` / `terraform plan` if the cluster does not yet exist. The two-phase apply is documented in prerequisites, but the provider configuration itself will cause evaluation errors during Phase-1 planning because Terraform evaluates all provider blocks before any resource targeting.

[HIGH] The OCIR image pull secret dependency is noted as a gap but is a hard prerequisite that will cause pod ImagePullBackOff failures if missed. The kubernetes_deployment references `ocir_pull_secret_name` but the secret is not created by Terraform — it requires a manual kubectl step between phases, making the deployment non-reproducible from Terraform alone.

[MEDIUM] The dynamic group matching rule is compartment-wide (matches ALL instances in the compartment), which is significantly broader than the ECS TaskExecutionRole that only applies to ECS tasks. This is acknowledged in gaps but represents a real security concern — any compute instance in the compartment gets the same permissions as the application pods.

[MEDIUM] The shape mapping 'PostgreSQL.VM.Standard.E4.Flex.2.32GB' (2 vCPU / 32 GB RAM) is significantly over-provisioned compared to db.t3.medium (2 vCPU / 4 GB RAM). While the CPU count matches, the memory is 8x larger, which will result in higher costs.

[LOW] The HTTPS listener is conditionally created based on ssl_certificate_name, but the original CloudFormation template has a listener on port 443. Without the HTTPS listener, the ALB NSG rule allowing port 443 ingress will accept traffic that has no listener to serve it.

[LOW] The flexible shape bandwidth is set to 10-100 Mbps, which may be insufficient for production workloads that the original ALB handled. AWS ALB scales automatically with no predefined bandwidth cap.

[LOW] The main.tf excerpt is truncated at the locals block. While the resource_mappings describe all resources comprehensively, the actual HCL for most resources (NSGs, DB system, OKE cluster, node pool, K8s resources, etc.) cannot be syntactically validated from what is provided.

[MEDIUM] The kubernetes_service is annotated to attach to the pre-created OCI LB and backend set, which requires OCI Cloud Controller Manager (CCM) specific annotations. If these annotations are incorrect or the CCM version doesn't support this pattern, the integration between the K8s service and the OCI LB may fail. This is a complex integration point that differs significantly from ECS-ALB tight coupling.

[LOW] The MQL query targets the oci_containerengine namespace, but for Virtual Node Pools the metric namespace and metric names may differ from standard OKE managed node pools. Virtual Nodes emit metrics through a different path.
